customer_anxiety_profile: Ashley is a high-skepticism customer with a legitimate grievance (Ion's contact volume) layered on top of general solar-scam anxiety. She consented to the appointment conditionally — she is NOT a warm lead. Her primary unresolved question is how Ion makes money if installation is free. She is intelligent, assertive, and will detect any inconsistency between what Meg described and what Sam presents.
unresolved_concerns: How does Ion generate revenue if installation is free? Ashley asked this directly at 14:58 and it was deferred entirely to Sam. This must be addressed in the first 2 minutes or Ashley will disengage.

Andy (co-title holder, husband) is excluded from this appointment by Ashley's explicit decision. Sam needs to acknowledge this is Ashley's choice while creating a natural opening for Andy's future involvement — do not push, but do not ignore.

Ashley's fear that 'this is going to bite people after they've signed up' (17:06) — a post-commitment regret concern. Sam should walk through what the contract actually looks like before any commitment discussion.
sensitive_topics_raised: Medical bills recently ('we've had a lot of medical bills lately' — 02:44). Credit concern is live even though Ashley self-assessed above 670. Sam should treat credit as unconfirmed.
Ashley expressed sustained frustration with Ion's contact volume across multiple points in the call. Sam must not reference how Ashley came to them in a way that reminds her of the email volume.

Ashley explicitly said she does not want to be harassed post-appointment: 'I would love it if y'all would stop trying to contact me' (18:58). Any follow-up contact before Monday must come from Meg's personal line only, as promised.
exact_promises_rep_made: Meg will be on the Monday 10:30 call personally — 'The only thing I would say is that if you're not on the line, I'm hanging up. K. I gotcha. I'll let him know.' (21:13). This is a hard condition Ashley set. If Meg cannot be on the call, Ashley must be notified before Monday.

Ashley has been removed from all email, text, and calling queues except Meg's personal line (confirmed multiple times across 01:45, 04:16, 19:44). Any automated contact before Monday will likely kill the appointment.

Sam will have 'things prepared' to give Ashley 'an exact clear answer on how it all works' financially (17:09). Sam cannot open with a generic pitch — Ashley is expecting specific financial transparency.
The appointment is Monday at 10:30, approximately 30 minutes, blocked for up to an hour.
closer_should_know: Ashley's appointment is conditioned on Meg being present on the call — confirm Meg's availability before Monday or call Ashley directly from Meg's personal line to reset expectations. If Sam opens without Meg, Ashley said she will hang up.
